hype.text: The favourites France roll into town with Bell clinging to them like a rip current — fitting, since the man who spent all winter indoors cataloguing insects is now one Mbappé masterclass away from £140 in a sport he openly doesn't like. Dom's Morocco are the 6/1 outsiders, but he's officially liked football for nearly three months now, so expect expert tactical analysis delivered through a haze of smoke and Aberdeen-flavoured tears.

Norway v England, and for once the group chat is fully united behind the Norwegians — Todd included, assuming he can put the guitar down for two hours on a Saturday night. If Haaland does to England's back line what Year 9 at St Mary's does to Todd every weekday, the £140 dream stays alive and nobody has to pretend to be happy for the Three Lions.

Friday night and Will's Spain are heavy favourites, which is awkward for a man who'd rather be grinding his PhD or heroically hurling himself under children on Prague slides than watching a football match. Darley's Belgium are the long shot, but rest assured he'll mention he lives in London before kickoff and miss both goals scanning the crowd cam.

A 2am kickoff suits Ethan down to the ground — it's the one hour of the day he's awake and technically not being unproductive, watching Messi's heirs chase the £140 his car finance is screaming for. Perez's Switzerland are outsiders, but the man's buying a house in a city he's never set foot in, so backing long odds sight unseen is frankly on brand — he'll watch it how he watched his childhood: sat under a desk.
